ques: Find a Coursera course on Sustainable Agriculture practices, and detail the course's objectives and the background of the lead instructor.
answer: Course — "Sustainable Agricultural Land Management" (University of Florida, Coursera). Objectives: covers Florida's agricultural/urban water-quality issues, land and nutrient management strategies, and the science and policy behind Best Management Practices (BMPs), teaching students to evaluate BMP research and its role in water-protecting policy; 8 modules spanning water quality regulations, TMDLs & nutrients, BMPs, soil management, nutrient management, water for agriculture, and irrigation management. Lead instructor — George J. Hochmuth, Ph.D., a Professor at the University of Florida who runs a research program in urban and agricultural fertilization and nutrient (nitrogen/phosphorus) and water-management practices, studying water quality, irrigation efficiency, and BMPs via greenhouse, field, and hydroponic research.
